title: marlierea schottii
intro: Marlierea schottii, also known as the "Pau-Brasil" tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This tree is native to the tropical regions of South America and has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries. The bark, leaves, and roots of the Marlierea schottii tree have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. In herbal preparations, Marlierea schottii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system.
